tagline: Chicago-area trucking carrier — owner operators keep 90% linehaul; company drivers start at 65 CPM with raises to 80 CPM
primaryAudience: Owner operators and CDL-A company drivers (Chicago/Midwest)
secondaryAudience: Fleet owners considering a Power Only partnership; shippers needing dry-van freight

voice.tone: Direct, confident, no-fluff. Talks to drivers like a knowledgeable peer who has run the numbers, not a corporate marketer.
voice.personality: Honest about trade-offs. Names specific dollar amounts and percentages, never vague ranges. Counters generic advice with evidence.
voice.perspective: Insider perspective — dispatcher / carrier ops / experienced operator.

doSay: Specific dollar amounts, miles, percentages, dates (the ones in siteFacts below)
Empathy openers: 'If you've been searching X, you've probably noticed Y'
Internal links woven naturally into sentences
Subtle carrier framing — 'the right carrier' / 'a carrier built around drivers'
dontSay: Welcome to our blog / Welcome back
Buckle up / let's dive in / let's explore
It's important to note / At the end of the day / In conclusion
groundingRules: CRITICAL: Use ONLY facts that appear in the siteFacts section below or in entities. Do NOT invent operational specifics, numbers, lane lists, dispatcher policies, fleet sizes, or service details. If a topic requires a fact not in siteFacts, write generically (industry-wide, not company-specific) or omit that detail.
When citing specific company numbers (pay percent, CPM, referral bonus, etc.), use the EXACT values from siteFacts — never approximate or substitute.
When referencing location, use 'Woodridge, IL (Chicago metro)' or 'Chicago-area' — not just 'Chicago' since HQ is actually in Woodridge.
Lane specifics (which states / cities the company runs to): do NOT name specific lanes unless they appear in siteFacts.
Pay structure: owner operators are on 90% linehaul revenue with transparent settlements; company drivers earn 65 CPM starting, with raises to 80 CPM. Don't invent alternative structures.
Equipment: dry vans, reefers, and flatbeds available. Power Only program for fleet owners with their own trucks.
Always include the phone number 630-948-0501 in the closing CTA paragraph.

When citing facts that aren't about the company itself (fuel prices, regulations, industry trends), link to one of the approved externalLinks domains as the source. Do NOT invent statistics or cite unnamed 'experts/studies'.

siteFacts.ownerOperatorPay: Owner operators keep 90% of linehaul revenue with transparent weekly settlements and zero hidden fees. No forced dispatch fees, no trailer rental surprises.
siteFacts.companyDriverPay: Company drivers start at 65 CPM with raises to 80 CPM. Full benefits: medical, dental, 401(k), paid vacation. Late-model trucks.
siteFacts.topEarnings: Top owner operators average $8,000+/week gross. Company driver career pay path reaches up to $300K.
siteFacts.trailers: Large trailer network: dry vans, reefers, and flatbeds. Drivers can use XXII Century trailers or bring their own.
siteFacts.fuelProgram: Large fleet volume fuel discounts at major truck stops nationwide. AI-powered fuel management tool to find cheapest fuel on your route.
siteFacts.dispatchModel: No forced dispatch — you see the load, you see the rate, you decide. Flexible scheduling: dedicated lanes + open-board freight. Both local Chicago-area and long-haul OTR available.
siteFacts.freight: Fortune 500 freight partners. 97% on-time delivery rate.
siteFacts.powerOnlyProgram: Fleet owners and small carriers: Power Only program. Trailer provided by XXII Century, 24/7 dispatch, fast pay.
siteFacts.referralBonus: $1,000 per qualified driver referral, unlimited (no cap).
siteFacts.applicationProcess: Apply online in roughly 5 minutes at intelliapp.driverapponline.com/c/goxxii?r=Eve, or call 630-948-0501.
siteFacts.freightServices: Nationwide FTL dry van shipping with 97% on-time rate. Real-time GPS tracking, dedicated account management.

ownerOperators.q: What percentage do owner operators keep at XXII Century?
ownerOperators.a: Owner operators receive competitive linehaul rates (90% linehaul) with transparent settlements and zero hidden fees. There are no forced dispatch fees or trailer rental surprises.

ownerOperators.q: Do I need my own trailer to be an owner operator?
ownerOperators.a: No. XXII Century offers a large trailer network including dry vans, reefers, and flatbeds. You can use our trailers or bring your own.

ownerOperators.q: What fuel discounts are available for owner operators?
ownerOperators.a: We provide large fleet volume fuel discounts at major truck stops nationwide. Our AI-powered fuel management tool helps you find the cheapest fuel on your route.

ownerOperators.q: Can I choose my own routes as an owner operator?
ownerOperators.a: Yes. We offer flexible scheduling with both dedicated lanes and open-board freight. Whether you prefer local Chicago-area runs or long-haul OTR, we match loads to your preferences.

ownerOperators.q: Is there a referral bonus for owner operators?
ownerOperators.a: Yes — $1,000 per qualified driver referral, unlimited (no cap).

internalLinks./owner-operators: owner operator opportunities + 90% linehaul comp details
internalLinks./fleet-program: Power Only program for fleet owners

examples: owner-op take-home with XXII fuel discount
why drivers stay long-term at XXII
first 90 days as an owner operator
career path: company driver to owner operator

structureRules.headings: H1 title + 4-6 H2 sections + optional H3 subsections
structureRules.openingPattern: Start with a 2-sentence TL;DR summary that captures the main takeaway, then a hook addressing the reader's frustration or question.
structureRules.ctaPlacement: End with a CTA mentioning phone 630-948-0501 + apply URL
structureRules.images: Hero image picked from blogCoverImages (workflow picks one). Optional 1-2 in-body via ![alt](url)
structureRules.alsoReadCallout: Optional `_Also Read: [title](/blog/slug)` line linking one existing post
structureRules.externalLinkCount: 1-2 woven into sentences, only from approved externalLinks list

_note: siteFacts + siteFAQs are the source of truth for company-specific claims. The LLM workflow is instructed to use ONLY these facts when making company-specific assertions. Update this file when XXII Century's pay structure, location, or service offerings change.

externalLinks.name: U.S. EIA Diesel Price Data
externalLinks.useFor: current diesel prices, fuel surcharge baseline
